name: Temporal profile of alphaH(a)
hypothesis: The TAUG parameter alphaH(a) follows a Fibonacci-Hirano profile alphaH(a) = (1/8)*4*a*(1-a) = a*(1-a)/2, which yields alphaH(a=0)=0, alphaH(a=0.5)=1/8 (peak), alphaH(a=1)=0, satisfying both Hulse-Taylor binary pulsar constraint at z=0 and CMB constraint at z=1100.

internal_inconsistency.issue: Paper TAUG v1.1 line 96 declares EFTCAMB RPH parametrization with MP0=0.014, BR0=0.005, exp=3
internal_inconsistency.implication: RPH gives alphaH(a)=MP0*a^exp, so alphaH(a=1)=0.014 NOT 0.125
internal_inconsistency.status: UNRESOLVED - paper has internal contradiction between Fibonacci profile (peak alphaH=0.125) and EFTCAMB RPH MP0=0.014
possible_resolutions: MP0 in paper uses non-standard normalization (must be specified)
9 MCMC chains have undetected normalization bug
Two different parametrizations are mixed in the paper without explicit reconciliation

falsification_criteria.name: Run EFTCAMB with taug_rph_br005.ini and check if recovers alphaH=0.125 peak or alphaH=0.014 max
Compare two profile predictions vs Planck CMB lensing
falsification_criteria.Profile_A_Fibonacci: Predicts eta(z=1100)~1, CMB lensing matches GR
falsification_criteria.Profile_B_RPH: Predicts eta(z=1100)~0, CMB lensing matches GR
falsification_criteria.comment: Both profiles give GR at CMB, so CMB cannot distinguish them
